The '''AP-1-C attack droid''' (Armored Platform, model 1, command) was a [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] used by the [[Imperial Remnant]]. It was created by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd]], makers of the [[Viper probe droid/Legends|Arakyd Viper]]. The AP-1-C was armed with a repeating [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], but more commonly was used, as its name suggests, as a commander for its ally [[AP-2 attack droid|AP-2]] and [[AP-3 attack droid]]s.<ref name="Cadinth" />

The attack droid saw combat during the [[Battle of Cadinth]].<ref name="Cadinth" />

==Behind the scenes==
The "Imperial Attack Droid B" model was designed for the abandoned ''[[The Epic Continues]]'' story line. The prototype model was made by kitbashing [[2-1B/Legends|2-1B]] and [[4-LOM/Legends|4-LOM]] figures, and was given [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]' gun.<ref name="Droid B">{{WebCite|url=http://theswca.com/index.php?action=disp_item&item_id=50970|text=1985-86 Line Extension Presentation Binder Page 15 - Imperial Attack Droid B|work=The Star Wars Collectors Archive|archivedate=20191122201806}}</ref>

The droid later appeared in the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] continuity where it was officially named the AP-1-C attack droid. The similarly-created AP-2 and AP-3 attack droids also appeared in the same source, along with other concepts from the rejected toy line.<ref name="Cadinth" />
